summary: Russian forces intensified aerial attacks, targeting Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy regions and striking a Turkish vessel en route to Turkey. Ukrainian units reported recent territorial gains in Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, while both sides increased UAV activity across multiple fronts. Regional incidents also involved Russian drones over Romania and a reported transport aircraft buildup at Kursk and Shaikivka airfields.
key_points: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @kpszsu/63258).
Russian attacks hit multiple Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy, with repeated strikes on a Sumy site (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
Ukrainian forces liberated Budarki, Karaichnoye, Novopidgorodne, and Lisnoye within 24 hours (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).
Ukrainian units cleared areas near Novoselivka and other villages, reclaiming at least 46 km² (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
Russian drone struck a Turkish ship, causing fire and two crew injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81527).
Russian drone crashed in Galaţi, Romania, causing fire and evacuating 70 residents (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).
Increased activity of AN‑72 and IL‑76MD‑90A transport aircraft heading to Kursk and Shaikivka airfields (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).

sections.executive_summary: Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Dnipropetrovsk, while Russian drones struck Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy and a Turkish vessel. Ukrainian forces reported recent liberations of villages in Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts, expanding control by at least 46 km². Transport aircraft activity suggests preparation for larger-scale operations, raising operational tension.

points: Ukrainian forces liberated Budarki and Karaichnoye in Kharkiv region and Novopidgorodne and Lisnoye in Dnipropetrovsk region within a 24‑hour period, according to the Ministry of Defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).

The General Staff reported successful operations on the Oleksandrivka sector, clearing enemy forces near Novoselivka and reclaiming at least 46 km² across multiple villages including Voronny, Sichneve, and Zelenyi Hai (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).

Ukrainian special forces neutralized a key FSB radio‑electronic intelligence site in the Krasnodar region, disabling enemy targeting and satellite signal interception capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81537).
Ukrainian air defenses engaged multiple UAV groups over Sumy, Poltava, and Chernihiv, reporting fewer than ten hostile drones remaining in the sky (Source: @eRadarrua/94229).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb strikes on Dnipropetrovsk region, indicating continued use of precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/63258).
Russian forces launched guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia region, expanding aerial attack patterns (Source: @kpszsu/63264).
Russian transport aircraft AN‑72 and IL‑76MD‑90A were observed preparing for landings at Kursk and Shaikivka airfields, suggesting buildup of additional strike assets (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).
Russian drones targeted Naftogaz infrastructure in Kharkiv and Sumy, with repeated strikes on a Sumy facility over several hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
UAV groups were reported moving from the sea towards the Zatoka area, indicating potential maritime‑to‑land strike vectors (Source: @eRadarrua/94246).

UAVs were observed heading from the north of Kharkiv towards Valky, and from the east of Dnipropetrovsk towards Ternevka, showing sustained drone activity across the eastern sector (Source: @eRadarrua/94231; @kpszsu/63262).
Russian guided bomb attacks on Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia highlight an intensified aerial campaign on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/63258; @kpszsu/63264).
Transport aircraft movements to Kursk and Shaikivka suggest preparation for larger operations in the eastern theater (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).
UAV groups were reported over northern Kharkiv heading towards Staryi Merchik, indicating northward pressure (Source: @kpszsu/63256).
UAV activity was noted over Poltava near Kotelva moving towards Gogolevo, showing north‑central movements (Source: @kpszsu/63242).
UAVs observed near Balaklia in eastern Kharkiv region suggest continued reconnaissance and strike capability in the north‑east (Source: @kpszsu/63268).
No direct reports of ground engagements on the northern front, but aerial activity remains elevated.
Russian drone struck a Turkish vessel traveling from Odesa to Turkey, causing fire and two crew injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81527).
A Russian drone entered Romanian airspace, crashing into a residential building in Galaţi, igniting a fire and prompting evacuation of 70 residents (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).
Ukrainian forces reported no missile flights over Kyiv at present, with only ballistic passes observed (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67275).

Multiple rocket alerts were issued for southern locations including Kremenchuk, Bila Tserkva, and Uman, indicating ongoing missile threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67265; @mon1tor_ua/67278; @mon1tor_ua/67267).
Russian tactical aviation employed guided aerial bombs in both Dnipropetrovsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, reflecting a coordinated air strike campaign (Source: @kpszsu/63258; @kpszsu/63264).
Ukrainian air defenses reported a reduction to fewer than ten hostile drones over Sumy, Poltava, and Chernihiv, suggesting effective interception (Source: @eRadarrua/94229).
Transport aircraft AN‑72 and IL‑76MD‑90A were observed preparing for landings at Kursk and Shaikivka, indicating potential reinforcement of air assets (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).
Russian drone incursions extended beyond Ukraine, striking a Turkish ship and a Romanian residential building, demonstrating cross‑border aerial reach (Source: @UkraineNow/81527; @UkraineNow/81524).

key_developments: Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Dnipropetrovsk region, marking an escalation in precision strike usage (Source: @kpszsu/63258).
Multiple Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy were attacked, with a Sumy site hit repeatedly over several hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
Ukrainian forces liberated Budarki, Karaichnoye, Novopidgorodne, and Lisnoye within a 24‑hour period, expanding control in Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30524).
Ukrainian units cleared areas near Novoselivka, reclaiming at least 46 km² across several villages (Source: @DeepStateUA/23544).
A Russian drone struck a Turkish vessel en route from Odesa to Turkey, causing fire and two crew injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81527).
A Russian drone crashed in Galaţi, Romania, igniting a fire and forcing evacuation of 70 residents (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).
AN‑72 and IL‑76MD‑90A transport aircraft were observed preparing to land at Kursk and Shaikivka airfields, suggesting buildup of additional strike capabilities (Source: @eRadarrua/94249).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial operations have intensified, employing guided bombs and expanding UAV incursions into Ukrainian and neighboring airspaces, indicating a strategy to pressure both military and civilian targets. Ukrainian territorial gains in the east demonstrate effective counter‑offensives, yet the presence of transport aircraft hints at potential escalation. Cross‑border drone attacks on Turkey and Romania raise the risk of broader regional involvement and complicate air defense priorities.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine's defense ministry has urged civilians to heed air‑raid alerts, especially in Kyiv and southern regions, while highlighting successful liberations. Neighboring states, notably Romania, have condemned drone violations and are coordinating diplomatic responses. International partners continue to monitor the situation, with NATO and EU officials discussing security implications.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of AN‑72/IL‑76 movements to Kursk and Shaikivka should be prioritized to anticipate possible large‑scale air operations. UAV activity patterns over Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and the north suggest coordinated reconnaissance ahead of ground actions. The repeated targeting of energy infrastructure underscores Russia's intent to disrupt logistics and civilian morale.
